content.body:
Vector: has magnitude AND direction
Scalar: magnitude only

Vector notation: a⃗, AB⃗, or column vector (x/y)

Addition: (a/b) + (c/d) = (a+c/b+d)
Subtraction: a⃗ - b⃗ = a⃗ + (-b⃗)
Scalar multiplication: k(a/b) = (ka/kb)

Magnitude: |a⃗| = √(x²+y²)
Unit vector: a⃗/|a⃗|

content.title: Position Vectors
content.body:
Position vector of point P(3,4): OP⃗=(3/4)
Vector AB⃗ = OB⃗ - OA⃗
Midpoint M of AB: OM⃗ = ½(OA⃗+OB⃗)

A(1,2), B(5,6):
AB⃗=(4/4), midpoint=(3/4)

questions.question: A vector has
questions.explanation: Vectors: magnitude + direction. Scalars (temperature, mass): magnitude only

questions.question: Unit vector of (0/5)=
questions.explanation: Magnitude=5, unit vector=(0/5)/5=(0/1)

questions.question: Midpoint of A(2,0) and B(6,4). Position vector of M=
Two vectors are equal if
options: they have same magnitude and direction
questions.answer: they have same magnitude and direction
questions.explanation: Equal vectors: same magnitude AND direction (can be at different positions — free vectors)

questions.question: Negative vector -a⃗ has
questions.explanation: Negative vector: reverses direction, keeps magnitude. Geometrically: vector pointing the other way

questions.question: Vector (a/b) is parallel to (2a/2b) because
options: one is scalar multiple of other (k=2)
questions.answer: one is scalar multiple of other (k=2)
questions.explanation: Parallel vectors are scalar multiples of each other. (2a/2b)=2(a/b)

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

content.body:
Parallel vectors: b⃗ = k·a⃗
Collinear points: AB⃗ = k·AC⃗

Dot product: a⃗·b⃗ = |a⃗||b⃗|cosθ
= x₁x₂ + y₁y₂
Perpendicular vectors: a⃗·b⃗ = 0

Vector parametric equation of line:
r⃗ = a⃗ + t·d⃗
a⃗=point on line, d⃗=direction vector, t=parameter

content.title: Geometric Proofs
content.body:
OABC parallelogram: OA⃗=a⃗, OC⃗=c⃗
OB⃗=a⃗+c⃗, CA⃗=a⃗-c⃗
Midpoint of OB: ½(a⃗+c⃗)
Midpoint of AC: ½(OA⃗+OC⃗)... wait=½(a⃗+c⃗)✓
Diagonals bisect each other!

Division: P divides AB in ratio m:n
OP⃗=(n·OA⃗+m·OB⃗)/(m+n)

content.title: Applications
content.body:
Velocity vectors: v⃗=(3/4) means 3m/s east, 4m/s north
Speed=|v⃗|=5m/s
Bearing: angle from north

Force vectors: resultant = vector sum
Equilibrium: sum of all forces = zero vector

questions.question: Dot product (2/3)·(4/1)=
questions.explanation: Dot product: x₁x₂+y₁y₂=2×4+3×1=8+3=11

questions.question: Vectors (1/2) and (2/-1) are
questions.explanation: Dot product: 1×2+2×(-1)=2-2=0 → perpendicular

questions.question: Vectors are parallel if one is
questions.explanation: Parallel: b=ka for some scalar k (same or opposite direction)

questions.question: Line through A(1,2) direction (3/4). Point on line when t=2
P divides AB in ratio 1:3. A=(0,0), B=(8,4). P=
Force (3/4)N and (1/-2)N act on object. Resultant magnitude =
Equilibrant of force (3/4) is
questions.explanation: Equilibrant = negative of resultant, brings system to equilibrium

questions.question: In parallelogram OABC, OA⃗=a and OC⃗=c. OB⃗=
questions.explanation: OB⃗=OA⃗+AB⃗=OA⃗+OC⃗=a+c (diagonal of parallelogram)

questions.question: Collinear points A,B,C: AB⃗=(2/4), AC⃗=(6/12). Are they collinear?
options: Yes, AC⃗=3×AB⃗ so A,B,C are collinear
questions.answer: Yes, AC⃗=3×AB⃗ so A,B,C are collinear
questions.explanation: (6/12)=3×(2/4) → same direction → A,B,C lie on the same straight line

content.body: In this lesson, we explore advanced aspects of Vectors 2D. Build on what you have already learned and apply your knowledge to more challenging problems.
Learning Objectives

By the end of this lesson you should be able to: (1) Apply Vectors 2D concepts to complex problems. (2) Use appropriate formulas and methods. (3) Interpret results in context. (4) Connect this topic to related areas of mathematics.
Core Theory

This section of Vectors 2D builds on foundational knowledge. In mathematics, advanced problem-solving requires not just knowing formulas but understanding when and how to apply them. Study each worked example carefully.

content.title: Worked Example 3.1
content.body: Consider a more complex problem on Vectors 2D. Approach: (1) Read and understand the problem. (2) Identify all given information. (3) Determine which concepts apply. (4) Solve systematically, showing all steps. (5) Check your answer is reasonable.

content.title: Worked Example 3.2
content.body: Another type of problem you may encounter: Questions may test your ability to combine multiple concepts within Vectors 2D. Practice identifying the type of problem and the appropriate method before attempting to solve.
Common examination questions on Vectors 2D include: definitions and explanations, numerical calculations, diagram interpretation, and analysis of given data. Make sure you can handle all types.
Summary and Review

You have now covered all lessons in Vectors 2D. Review the key concepts from each lesson, practice past paper questions, and ensure you can apply your knowledge confidently. Good luck in your studies!

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area of a rectangle = length × width.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a like term with 3x²?
questions.explanation: Like terms must have the same variable AND the same power. 3x has power 1, not 2, so it is not a like term with 3x².

questions.question: What is the gradient/slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has no rise, so gradient = rise/run = 0/run = 0.

questions.question: If a = 4 and b = 3, find 2a + b.
questions.explanation: Substitute: 2(4) + 3 = 8 + 3 = 11.

questions.question: What does the inequality x > 5 mean?
questions.explanation: The > symbol means 'greater than'. x > 5 means x takes values greater than (but not equal to) 5.

questions.question: Solve: 2(x + 3) = 14
questions.explanation: Expand: 2x + 6 = 14. Subtract 6: 2x = 8. Divide: x = 4.

questions.question: The HCF of 12 and 18 is:
questions.explanation: Factors of 12: 1,2,3,4,6,12. Factors of 18: 1,2,3,6,9,18. Highest common factor = 6.

questions.question: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4,8,12,... Multiples of 6: 6,12,... Lowest common multiple = 12.

questions.question: Which number is prime?
questions.explanation: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself. 7 is prime (factors: 1 and 7 only).
